intro: Ericoides ciliare, also known as Javanese catechu or acacia catechu, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ericoides ciliare include catechin, gallic acid, and other flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
